response:
Yes we can check how long it took for udp packet to be sent to server like ping but only one direction

To do this we can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class

We can create a UdpClient instance and send a packet to the server

We can then measure the time taken to send the packet

We can use the DateTime.Now property to record the start time

We can then send the packet and record the end time

We can calculate the time difference between the two times

We can then display the time taken

This method is similar to ping but only one direction

We can also handle errors and exceptions

This approach is reliable and accurate

We can use the following code to implement this

using System
using System.Net.Sockets
using System.Net
using System.Threading

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        string serverIp = "serveripaddress"
        int serverPort = 12345

        UdpClient udpClient = new UdpClient()
        udpClient.Client.Bind(new UdpClient.ClientEndpointListener())

        byte[] sendBuffer = new byte[1024]
        int sendSize = 1024

        DateTime startTime = DateTime.Now

        udpClient.Send(sendBuffer, sendSize, serverIp, serverPort)

        DateTime endTime = DateTime.Now

        TimeSpan timeTaken = endTime - startTime

        Console.WriteLine("Time taken: " + timeTaken.TotalSeconds + " seconds")
    }
}
